findings.blocker: Four transient sorries remain inside the theorem body: (a) original substep a at L444, (a0) substep a for finite cover s0 at L690, (i) Cech refinement transport s -> s0 at L662, (b2+c) finite product-localisation + exact_of_isLocalized_span on s0 at L720.

attempts.strategy: Decompose the monolithic substep (b)+(c) sorry into three independent transient sorries with structured scaffolding: h_transport (Cech refinement transport), h_K0_exact (exactness of finite-cover complex), and h_a0 (extra-degeneracy for s0-indexed slice cover). Close original goal via exact h_transport.mpr h_K0_exact.

attempts.insight: The exact h_transport.mpr h_K0_exact pattern decouples the transport proof from the subgoal proof. The finite-cover complex K0 is now the primary target, with transport back to K handled by a bidirectional equivalence.

next_steps: Component (i) h_transport is the most self-contained piece — prove Cech cohomology depends only on the generated sieve, or construct an explicit quasi-isomorphism K -> K0. Component (ii) h_K0_exact can be attacked independently: install Module instances on K0.X i (finite product, pointwise module structure), then use exact_of_isLocalized_span on s0. Substep (a) for s0 (h_a0) is a near-verbatim copy of original substep (a) with slice cover indexed by s0 instead of s.

attempts.strategy: Define relativeDifferentialsPresheaf directly via PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' applied to the inverse-image presheaf morphism f.c converted via the pullback-pushforward adjunction.
attempts.code_tried: noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules := let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c; PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
attempts.insight: The pullback-pushforward adjunction homEquiv converts the scheme morphism's structure sheaf map f.c : S.presheaf ⟶ pushforward.obj X.presheaf into the form pullback.obj S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf needed by relativeDifferentials'.

next_steps: Close relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (L67) — the sheaf condition for Kaehler differentials follows from localization compatibility.

attempts.strategy: Package the presheaf with the sheaf condition theorem into X.Modules via the tuple constructor.
attempts.code_tried: noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules := ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩
attempts.insight: Standard sheaf-upgrade pattern: pair a PresheafOfModules with a Presheaf.IsSheaf proof to obtain an X.Modules object.

findings.blocker: Sheaf condition proof requires showing Kaehler differentials commute with localization on affine opens. Mathlib may already have lemmas about KaehlerDifferential commuting with localization.

attempts.strategy: State theorem with sorry body as placeholder for sheaf condition proof.
attempts.code_tried: theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by sorry
attempts.insight: The sheaf condition is the first priority for Phase B proof closure. It follows from the fact that Kaehler differentials commute with localization: Omega_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Omega_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f].

next_steps: Search Mathlib for KaehlerDifferential localization lemmas (e.g., KaehlerDifferential.map, KaehlerDifferential.baseChange).

next_steps: Explore PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' and related API for converting a Derivation' to a presheaf morphism.

findings.blocker: Substantial theorem depending on ring-level cotangent exact sequence in Mathlib (Algebra.Generators.CotangentSpace.exact) and glueing across affine charts.

attempts.strategy: State theorem as existence of morphisms alpha and beta with ShortComplex.Exact and Epi beta. Use by sorry for both the main proof and the ShortComplex.mk composition proof.
attempts.code_tried: theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) : ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g)) (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f), CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β (by sorry)) ∧ CategoryTheory.Epi β := by sorry
attempts.goal_after: Theorem stated with two sorry bodies
attempts.insight: The statement captures the standard cotangent exact sequence f^* Omega_{Y/S} -> Omega_{X/S} -> Omega_{X/Y} -> 0. The by sorry inside ShortComplex.mk is a separate proof obligation (showing alpha ≫ beta = 0).

next_steps: Defer until relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf and universalDerivation are closed. Then build from affine charts using Mathlib's ring-level cotangent exact sequence.

findings.blocker: Substantial theorem depending on ring-level standard smoothness characterisation (RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension) and Nakayama's lemma.

attempts.strategy: State theorem as Smooth f ↔ forall x, exists affine open U containing x such that sections of relativeDifferentials over U are free of rank n over sections of O_X over U.
attempts.code_tried: theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S) (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) : Smooth f ↔ ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U); let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U); Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by sorry
attempts.insight: Uses the non-deprecated AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth (not IsSmooth). The statement matches the mathematical definition: smoothness of finite-presentation morphisms is equivalent to local freeness of Omega.

next_steps: Defer until ring-level results in Mathlib are mapped to the sheaf setting. Priority after sheaf condition and universal derivation.

findings.blocker: Corollary of smooth_iff_locally_free_omega via pullback along a section.

attempts.strategy: State corollary: if f is smooth and s is a section, then s^* Omega_{X/S} is locally free of rank n.
attempts.code_tried: theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S) (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ) (hsmooth : Smooth f) : ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U); let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U); Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by sorry
attempts.insight: Direct corollary of smooth_iff_locally_free_omega. Will close automatically once the main theorem is proven.
